raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding Citi/Visas handling of my credit card dispute for a coerced airline charge of {$3200.00} with XXXX XXXX ( Dispute ID : XXXX ). XXXX XXXX forced me to repurchase a ticket at the airport despite my original ticket being fully paid and confirmed. Their own written responses contradict each other and support that I was denied boarding without cause. 

I filed a dispute with Citi on XXXX. Citi initially opened the dispute but then closed it in error without contacting the merchant, which caused the charge to be re-applied to my account. When I provided additional documentation, Citi reopened the dispute, but they did not restore the conditional credit or properly code the charge as in dispute. 

Instead, Citis system is treating the disputed amount as an active owed balance, which is : generating a bill, reporting to credit bureaus, and harming my credit and my authorized users credit. 

This violates my protections under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) because : 1. A creditor can not bill or attempt to collect a charge under dispute ; 2. A creditor must prevent or correct adverse credit reporting on a disputed amount ; 3. A reopened dispute should be handled the same as an initial dispute ; and 4. A consumer can not be penalized for a creditors internal coding errors. 

What I have done : I contacted Citi multiple times. Citi admits the dispute is active and being reviewed, but they refuse to : put a conditional credit back on the account, or correct the coding to remove the balance from billing/credit reporting. 

This has caused documented credit score reduction for my authorized user and ongoing financial stress due to an incorrect balance that never should have appeared during an active dispute.

What I am requesting : 1. Reinstate the conditional credit for the {$3200.00} disputed charge ; 2. Correct all credit bureau reporting to show the charge as in dispute and not owed ; 3. Prevent further billing or interest on the disputed amount ; 4. Ensure Citis dispute review adheres to FCBA timelines and protections. 

I am not requesting a decision from the CFPB regarding the underlying merchant dispute only that Citi comply with federal billing laws and correct the administrative errors affecting my account and credit.
